text: Okay um, thanks everyone for joining the sync, I know it's a bit early so I appreciate you all being here on time.
So uh the main thing I wanted to walk through today is basically the Q1 roadmap and where we are on the platform reliability work.
First thing, on the incident side, we filed twenty three incident reports last quarter, which is honestly a little higher than I would've liked but most of them were minor.
Out of those twenty three, only four were P1, and two of those were the same downstream dependency, that auth gateway thing we keep talking about.
Right, yeah, and I mean, you know, we've been kind of putting that off for like two sprints now, so I think we really need to actually like, prioritize it this cycle.
Agreed. So on that note, the deliverables for the gateway migration are due March fourteenth, that's the hard deadline, no slippage on that one because of the compliance review.
Sorry, did you say March fourteenth or fourteenth of April? I just want to be super clear on which one.
March, March fourteenth, yeah. Pi day, easy to remember.
On the budget side, um, we're tracking about eleven percent over on the infra line, mostly from the GPU spend on the new model evaluation pipeline.

To go above the cap we technically need an exception, and the only person who can sign off on a budget exception over ten percent is Marcus Chen, our CFO. Nobody else, not even the VP of Eng can approve that.
Got it, so I'll, um, I'll loop in Marcus on the thread today. I think if we frame it as a one time spend tied to the migration deadline he'll probably be amenable.
Last thing, we're hiring two more SREs to backfill the team. Job recs are open, please uh, please share them in your networks if you can.
Okay I think that's everything. Thanks again everyone, let's um, let's reconvene next Tuesday, same time.
